description_long: This is a hand tool with a cylindrical, contoured handle made of red plastic, designed for ergonomic grip and comfort during use. The handle features subtle grooves and indentations for improved handling and slip resistance. Extending from the handle is a long, straight metallic shaft that terminates in a flat, wedge-shaped tip, characteristic of a flathead screwdriver. The shaft appears to be made of steel, providing durability and strength for turning screws. The overall construction is typical of general-purpose screwdrivers found in garages, workshops, and urban maintenance settings. No visible branding or markings are present on the handle or shaft.
description: A red-handled, contoured flathead screwdriver with a steel shaft and no visible branding. The handle is ergonomic and the tip is flat and wedge-shaped.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat, wedge-shaped tip and the circular end of the red handle.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the screwdriver, highlighting the contoured red handle and straight steel shaft.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the handle, with the shaft receding away from view.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the handle's contours and the straight shaft.
attribute_handle_type: contoured, straight, red plastic handle with ergonomic grooves
